title: xkcd 1313: Regex Golf (Part 2: Infinite Problems)

title: Letter Frequencies for Scrabble
summary: An analysis of frequency counts for letters in English, with applications to the game of Scrabble. Also presents a distillation of the Google Books Ngram data, broken out by time periods.

title: Photos of Costa Rica Wildlife
summary: Photos from a trip to Costa Rica with wildlife photographer Suzi Eszterhas.

title: English Letter Frequency Counts: Mayzner Revisited or ETAOIN SRHLDCU
summary: An analysis of frequency counts for letters and letter sequences (n-grams), with counts broken out by length of word and poition in word, as well as by length of ngram. A re-implementation of the work done by Mark Mayzner in the 1960s -- but this time with 30 million times more data.

title: FAQ for the 2012 US Presidential Election (#FF00FF)
summary: Added questions about early voting and state/federal to my frequently-asked-question list for the 2012 United States Presidential Election; all questions are answered with facts that are as objective as I can make them, except for the last question, in which I endorse President Obama.

title: Intro to Statistics
summary: This is not actually mine; it is a class by my co-teacher, Sebastian Thrun, on Intro to Statistics. I mention it because I think statistics is one of the most under-appreciated topics; everyone should learn statistics!

summary: My talk at the TED conference. Six minute video.

title: Google's Hybrid Approach to Research
summary: An article printed in the Communications of the ACM describing Google's research, both within Google Research and within Engineering as a whole. Alfred Spector, Peter Norvig and Slav Petrov.

title: Prescient but Not Perfect: A Look Back at a 1966 Scientific American Article on Systems Analysis

summary: The source code to accompany my essay on Strachey's Scientific American article.

title: Introduction to Artificial Intelligence
summary: Sebastian Thrun and I are teaching an AI class online; 126,000 people have signed up so far.

title: On Chomsky and the Two Cultures of Statistical Learning
summary: At the MIT 150 symposium, Chomsky said that the notion of success used by statistical language models (namely, that they successfully predict the world and allow programs to accomplish tasks) was "very novel ... I don't know of anything like it in the history of science." This essay argues that it is not novel at all, but perfectly commonplace.

title: Dance Photography
summary: An overview of photographic techniques for taking pictures of indoor dance performances. (Also useful for any indoor action photography.)
